content: Comparative analysis of intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM) and random network distillation (RND) for curiosity-driven reinforcement learning can be reframed as a study of how an agent’s intrinsic self-model shapes its exploratory memory formation, where the performance gap between ICM and RND maps directly to differences in how an agent encodes surprising, novel experiences into long-term active memory. This framework suggests that curiosity methods are not just exploration bonuses, but inherent mechanisms for structuring an agent’s active learning memory hierarchy.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
